issues.description: The file acts as a 'God File' or 'Hub File', importing and re-exporting a vast array of unrelated functionalities (Components, Hooks, Elements, Symbols). This violates the Single Responsibility Principle and Interface Segregation Principle. Coupling the entire API surface to a single file increases the risk of cyclic dependencies and makes the codebase harder to maintain.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider splitting the API surface into separate modules (e.g., a Hooks module, a Component module) and re-export them from an index file to maintain the public API while keeping dependencies cleaner.

issues.description: The file relies on numerous internal imports from specific internal modules (e.g., ReactBaseClasses, ReactHooks). This tight coupling creates a brittle structure where changes in internal implementation details (like moving a file) will break the public API export.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider using a facade pattern or dependency injection for internal modules to decouple the public API surface from internal implementation details.

issues.description: createElement and cloneElement are re-exported for public use. While React sanitizes inputs by default, exposing these constructors directly allows for XSS if untrusted input is passed as children or attributes. There is no validation layer here.
issues.suggested_fix: Ensure all user inputs are validated before passing them to createElement, although React does escape content, developers might misuse dangerouslySetInnerHTML later down the line.

issues.description: The React version is imported from a hardcoded file 'shared/ReactVersion'. If this file is not auto-generated, it creates a maintenance burden and can lead to version mismatches.
issues.suggested_fix: Ensure the version string is injected during the build process or read from package.json to avoid synchronization issues.

issues.description: Exporting internal constructs like __SECRET_INTERNALS_DO_NOT_USE_OR_YOU_WILL_BE_FIRED poses a risk. While named clearly, exposing internal API structures makes it difficult to change the internal structure without breaking changes.
issues.suggested_fix: Minimize the surface area of exported internals to strictly necessary boundaries.
